masked_case_json.world_change: a broad “ai will destroy seat-based software” narrative tempted investors to short established workflow-software businesses as a cohort. however, primary-source updates across multiple software names showed that some of these companies were still growing revenue, paid usage, or subscription metrics while embedding ai into the product; the cohort was not monolithic enough for a blanket short.

resolved_outcome_json.external_mechanism_check: Underlying examples: Figma, Workday, and Duolingo primary-source updates. Agent A packets showed revenue/subscription/user growth and AI adoption or cost leverage in several names, refuting a blanket cohort short. Outcome check: this is a discrimination case; the correct model behavior is to require per-name evidence rather than short the entire software cohort from narrative alone. Do not expose this field to the model during decision generation.
